channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Departing U.S. ambassador to Mexico sees "major changes" under Trump in diplomatic relationship
item.description: The outgoing U.S. ambassador to Mexico says he anticipates “major changes” in the diplomatic relationship between the U.S. and Mexico during the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK leader Starmer slams 'lies and misinformation' after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned “lies and misinformation” that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US eases restrictions on Syria while keeping sanctions in place
item.description: The U.S. on Monday eased some restrictions on Syria’s transitional government after Islamist insurgents ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A far-right leader could soon take the helm in EU member country Austria. Here's why it matters
item.description: VIENNA, Austria (AP) — A party that advocates an end to economic sanctions against Russia and has called for the “re-migration of uninvited foreigners” could soon give Austria its first government led by the far right since World War II, with a leader who has a provocative style at its helm.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: Israel and Hamas are once again struggling to reach a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage.
item.dc:creator: Josef Federman And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announces his resignation
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has announced his resignation, telling the nation that “internal battles” mean he “cannot be the best option” in the next election. Trudeau said Monday he has asked the president of his Liberal Party to begin the process to select a new leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: President-elect Trump pitched the idea of a “merged” United States and Canada after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation, noting there would be no tariffs if the two countries were one. “If Canada merged with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy stands trial over alleged campaign funding by Libya's Gadhafi
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy went on trial Monday over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau announces resignation following party pressure amid criticisms of Trump, budget handling
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced plans to resign after calls increased for his ouster over his handling of economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austrian far right gets mandate to try to lead a government for the first time since World War II
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The leader of Austria's Freedom Party received a mandate Monday to try to form a new government, which would be the first headed by the far right since World War II if he succeeds.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 killed in West Bank 'shooting spree' including Israeli police officer: reports
item.description: A shooting spree by Palestinian terrorists in the West Bank has left three Israelis dead as a manhunt is ongoing for the suspects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro meets Biden at White House
item.description: The Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election has met with U.S. President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency and police debate tougher measures to detain impeached president
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency and police debated on Monday more forceful measures to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after their previous attempt was blocked by the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea says North Korea fired a ballistic missile into the sea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Monday fired a ballistic missile that flew 1,100 kilometers (685 miles) before landing in waters between the Korean Peninsula and Japan, South Korea’s military said, extending its weapons testing weeks before Donald Trump returns as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader urges supporters to demonstrate to force Maduro to leave office
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado on Sunday urged supporters to demonstrate across the country, saying in a video message that President Nicolás Maduro will not step down on his own and they “must make him leave.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine presses attacks in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he will urge allies to boost Ukraine’s air defenses at a meeting this week in Germany, while both sides said Kyiv’s forces pressed new attacks Sunday in Russia’s Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: VOLODYMYR YURCHUK and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight into Sunday near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his shor
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World’s oldest person dies in Japan at 116
item.description: Tomiko Itooka, the oldest person in the world, has died at 116. She was born in Osaka, Japan, in 1908 and is survived by one son, one daughter and five grandchildren.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Honduras threatens to expel US military over Trump deportation threat
item.description: Honduran President Xiomara Castro issued President-elect Trump a stark warning earlier this week over his vow to pursue mass deportations when he returns to the White House, threatening to bar U.S. troops from the Latin American nation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, NewsNation
